sources.label: NKT News & Press Releases (latest item May 13, 2026: Q1 record order intake, EBITDA EUR 97m)
TS2: NKT shares hit fresh high as record cable orders reframe the 2026 story (post-Q1, mid-May)
ad-hoc-news: NKT record EUR 13.5B order backlog keeps power cable specialist in focus

verdicts.rankRationale: No company news since June 6; the Q1 beat and record EUR 13.5B backlog were already in yesterday's hold. The ~5% drift off the mid-May 52-week high (1,049 -> 995) with the thesis intact marginally improves entry asymmetry, but NKT remains a grid/TSO name one step removed from the AI passive/power bottleneck stack this page rewards, so rank 63 stands.
verdicts.bull: Record EUR 13.5B high-voltage backlog with multi-year visibility, Q1 EBITDA beat (EUR 97m vs EUR 84m consensus, 16.0% margin), and emerging data-center power-connection work (AKEA Denmark) give cheap optionality on AI-driven grid buildout while the pullback resets the entry.
verdicts.neutral: Over 95% of the Transmission backlog is European TSO interconnector/offshore-wind work, so AI/data-center exposure is real but indirect; +20.75% YTD with the post-Q1 rerating already done leaves the setup fairly priced for this list.
verdicts.bear: Crowding into the European HV-cable scarcity trade (NKT alongside Prysmian/Nexans) plus capex-heavy capacity expansion and project-execution risk means any backlog conversion slip or TSO timeline delay hits a stock that has already rerated.
verdicts.primaryCatalyst: Q2 2026 report (Aug 14) and incremental data-center/grid connection awards converting AI power demand into direct order intake.
verdicts.primaryRisk: Execution or timing slippage on the EUR 13.5B backlog, or sector de-crowding in European cable names, compressing the multiple without thesis news.
